destinations.evidence: The Nitro sigver precompile passes the executor-compatible 20 zero-byte nonce hex string to verify_nitro_attestation_raw instead of an empty nonce.

equivalence_argument: The precompile path decodes attestation/input data and invokes ritual-nsm verification with the exact 40-character zero hex nonce, matching the executor convention for a 20-byte zero nonce.
notes: Mapped in nodebuilder sigver precompile implementation.
rationale: The diff changes the nonce argument from an empty string to the 20-byte zero nonce hex string and comments that this matches the executor nonce. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The sigver Nitro attestation precompile path must call verify_nitro_attestation_raw with nonce hex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000, matching the executor's 20 zero-byte nonce convention, rather than an empty nonce.
suggested_falsification_tests: Verify a Nitro attestation produced with the executor's 20 zero-byte nonce through the sigver precompile and assert it succeeds; repeat with behavior equivalent to an empty nonce and assert the user-data/nonce verification no longer matches the executor-produced attestation.
summary: Nitro sigver uses the executor-compatible 20 zero-byte nonce.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Verify an attestation produced with a 20 zero-byte nonce through the Nitro sigver precompile and assert it succeeds; an empty-nonce attestation should not match the current verification call.
title: Nitro sigver uses zero-byte executor nonce
